Ahrar al-Sham.description: Ahrar al-Sham was a Sunni Islamist rebel group that fought against the Ba'athist regime of Bashar al-Assad during the Syrian Civil War. Formed in 2011, it grew to become one of the largest opposition factions, with an estimated 20,000 fighters at its peak. The group aimed to establish an Islamic state under Sharia law and was supported by Turkey, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia. It dissolved in January 2025, merging into Syria's new Ministry of Defense.

United Nations (gang).description: The United Nations (UN) is a criminal gang that originated in Abbotsford, British Columbia, Canada, founded in 1997 by Clayton Roueche and a group of high-school friends. The gang is involved in arms trafficking, drug trafficking, extortion, illegal immigration, money laundering, murder, and other crimes. It operates primarily in Canada and the United States, with alliances including the Triads of Vancouver and the Sinaloa Cartel.

PDC (gang).description: The PDC was a street gang based in Brixton, London, England, primarily active in the 1990s. They were involved in drug trafficking, robberies, and violence, and were the largest gang in the SW9 postcode. The gang later reformed as a music collective and label, while a faction broke off to form the 'Muslim Boys'.

Hells Angels Motorcycle Club.description: The Hells Angels Motorcycle Club is an international outlaw biker gang that expanded to Australia in 1975, establishing chapters in Melbourne and Sydney. It has approximately 250 members and 14 chapters in Australia, involved in drug trafficking, violent crimes, and money laundering through legitimate businesses. The club is legally classified as a criminal organization in Queensland and has been involved in conflicts with rival gangs such as the Comancheros and Bandidos.
